requirements: The core contributions of the paper 'CacheFlow: Efficient LLM Serving with 3D-Parallel KV Cache Restoration' have been reproduced.

sub_tasks.requirements: The base serving infrastructure (vLLM + LMCache) and required models are available for building CacheFlow on top of.
Code has been implemented to install and run vLLM as the underlying LLM serving engine that CacheFlow extends.

verification.spec: Verify that vLLM is installed and importable (e.g., `import vllm; vllm.__version__`) and that CacheFlow's serving entrypoint instantiates a vLLM engine (e.g., LLMEngine or AsyncLLMEngine) as its backend. Check by importing the CacheFlow module and asserting the underlying engine type is from vllm.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented to install and integrate LMCache v0.3.1 as the KV cache offloading layer used by CacheFlow.

verification.spec: Verify that lmcache==0.3.1 is installed (`pip show lmcache` reports version 0.3.1) and that CacheFlow imports lmcache modules for KV cache offloading. Assert the CacheFlow KV-cache adapter references lmcache classes (e.g., LMCacheEngine or similar).

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented to obtain and load the Qwen3-8B model for serving experiments.

verification.spec: Verify that the Qwen3-8B model can be downloaded from HuggingFace and loaded by the CacheFlow serving entrypoint. Run a smoke test that loads `Qwen/Qwen3-8B` via the configured engine and asserts a forward pass on a short prompt succeeds.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented to obtain and load the Llama-3.1-8B model for serving experiments.

verification.spec: Verify that the Llama-3.1-8B model can be downloaded from HuggingFace and loaded by the CacheFlow serving entrypoint. Run a smoke test that loads `meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B` via the configured engine and asserts a forward pass on a short prompt succeeds.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented to obtain and load the Qwen3-30B-A3B (MoE with 3B active experts) model for serving experiments.

verification.spec: Verify that the Qwen3-30B-A3B MoE model can be downloaded and loaded by the CacheFlow serving entrypoint. Run a smoke test that loads `Qwen/Qwen3-30B-A3B`, asserts config.num_experts > 1 and 3B active parameters, and runs a short forward pass.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented to use FlashAttention kernels (with block size aligned to 512 tokens) for both prefill recomputation and chunked KV restoration.

verification.spec: Inspect the CacheFlow attention/prefill kernel call sites and assert FlashAttention is used (e.g., `flash_attn` import or vllm's flash-attn backend selected). Assert the chunked-restoration config has chunk size C == 512 by default, matching FlashAttention's block size.

sub_tasks.requirements: Workload datasets used in the evaluation have been acquired and preprocessed into request traces with cached prefixes.
Code has been implemented to load the LMSYS-Chat-1M dataset and construct multi-turn conversational traces where successive turns share long prefixes.

verification.spec: Import the dataset-loading module and invoke its LMSYS-Chat loader. Verify it returns a list of conversation traces where each trace is multi-turn (>=2 turns) and successive turns share a long prefix (turn_i.prompt startswith turn_{i-1}.prompt+turn_{i-1}.response or equivalent reuse structure). Check that >=80% of sampled traces exhibit this prefix-sharing property and that at least some traces have prefixes >1000 tokens.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented to load the WildChat dataset and construct open-domain conversational request traces with diverse prefix lengths.

verification.spec: Import the dataset-loading module and invoke its WildChat loader. Verify it returns conversational request traces and check that prefix-length distribution is diverse (e.g., reports a histogram with both short (<500 tok) and long (>5000 tok) prefixes). Confirm the dataset source is allenai/WildChat or equivalent.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented to load the SWE-Bench dataset and construct agentic-pipeline request traces with repeated tool invocations over shared repository contexts.

verification.spec: Import the dataset-loading module and invoke its SWE-Bench loader. Verify it returns agentic-pipeline traces with repeated tool invocations and a shared repository context prefix across invocations within each task. Confirm at least one trace shows the same repository-context prefix shared across >=2 tool calls.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented to identify, for each request in each workload, the cached prefix length N_c (the prior shared context whose KV cache must be restored) and the uncached suffix to be prefilled fresh.

verification.spec: Construct a synthetic request whose prompt is [shared_prefix_tokens] + [new_suffix_tokens], with shared_prefix_tokens cached previously. Call the request-preprocessing function and assert it returns N_c equal to len(shared_prefix_tokens) and an uncached_suffix equal to new_suffix_tokens. Repeat with N_c=0 and full-cache cases to verify boundary handling.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented to pre-populate the external KV cache store (e.g., CPU memory / remote node simulated via LMCache) with KV tensors for the cached prefix of every request, so restoration experiments measure I/O of a real cached state.

verification.spec: Run the experiment-setup script that pre-populates the LMCache (or equivalent) external store with KV tensors for each request's prefix. After setup, query the store and verify that for every request r in the workload, KV tensors exist for all L layers covering tokens [0, N_c). Then start a restoration run and confirm I/O bytes are actually transferred (>0) during restoration.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented to characterize the input sequence length distribution of LMSYS-Chat, WildChat, and SWE-Bench (Figure 1a), confirming the prevalence of long input prefixes (e.g., requests exceeding ~20k tokens).

verification.spec: Run the length-characterization script on LMSYS-Chat, WildChat, and SWE-Bench. For each, output the CDF of input sequence lengths. Assert each CDF reaches at least 20000 tokens at some percentile (matching Figure 1a) and that a non-trivial fraction (>5%) of requests exceed 10k tokens.

sub_tasks.requirements: Hardware and network bandwidth conditions used by the experiments have been set up.
Code has been implemented or infrastructure provisioned to run CacheFlow on NVIDIA H100 (80 GB) GPUs as the default evaluation platform.

verification.spec: Verify that `nvidia-smi` reports at least one NVIDIA H100 80GB GPU and that CacheFlow's default launch configuration runs end-to-end on it (e.g., load Qwen3-8B and serve one request). Assert torch.cuda.get_device_name(0) contains 'H100'.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented or infrastructure provisioned to run CacheFlow on NVIDIA A100 (40 GB) GPUs for hardware-ablation experiments.

verification.spec: Verify that `nvidia-smi` reports at least one NVIDIA A100 40GB GPU and that CacheFlow's launch configuration runs end-to-end on it. Assert torch.cuda.get_device_name(0) contains 'A100'.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented or infrastructure provisioned to run CacheFlow on NVIDIA L40S (46 GB) GPUs (including 2xL40S deployments) for hardware-ablation experiments.

verification.spec: Verify that `nvidia-smi` reports at least two NVIDIA L40S 46GB GPUs and that CacheFlow's multi-GPU launch configuration (pipeline-stage=2) runs end-to-end on them. Assert torch.cuda.device_count() >= 2 and device names contain 'L40S'.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented to throttle/configure the KV cache I/O channel to a default bandwidth of 10 Gbps (Amazon Cloud inter-node bandwidth regime).

verification.spec: Verify that the KV-cache I/O channel can be configured to a bandwidth limit of 10 Gbps. Run a microbenchmark transferring a known-size KV blob through the offload path and assert the effective throughput is within 10% of 10 Gbps (e.g., transfer 1.25 GB and assert wall time is ~1.0s ± 0.1s). Also assert the bandwidth knob exists as a CLI/config flag set to 10 Gbps by default.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented to throttle/configure the KV cache I/O channel to 40 Gbps (typical Lambda Lab SSD / AWS g5.12xlarge inter-node) for bandwidth ablation.

verification.spec: Invoke the bandwidth-throttling configuration with target=40Gbps. During a KV-load operation, measure achieved bandwidth (bytes transferred / wall time) and assert it is approximately 40 Gbps (within 10%).

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented to throttle/configure the KV cache I/O channel to 80 Gbps (typical Infiniband / GCP a2-ultragpu-8g inter-node) for bandwidth ablation.

verification.spec: Invoke the bandwidth-throttling configuration with target=80Gbps. During a KV-load operation, measure achieved bandwidth (bytes transferred / wall time) and assert it is approximately 80 Gbps (within 10%).

sub_tasks.requirements: The CacheFlow 3D-parallel KV cache restoration method has been implemented.
Token-wise parallelism (one of the two 2D axes) has been implemented as described in Section 3.1.

Code has been implemented to partition the cached prefix of length N_c into ceil(N_c / C) contiguous chunks of size C, with C aligned to the FlashAttention preferred block size (typically 512 tokens).

verification.spec: Call the chunking utility with N_c=2050 and C=512; assert it returns ceil(2050/512)=5 chunks with indices [0..4], chunk sizes [512,512,512,512,2], and that the C value matches the FlashAttention block size constant in the code.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented to initialize a compute pointer at chunk 0 and an I/O pointer at chunk ceil(N_c / C) - 1, advancing the compute pointer forward (recomputing chunks 0,1,2,...) and retreating the I/O pointer backward (loading chunks from external storage).

verification.spec: Instantiate the token-wise two-pointer restoration object for a synthetic request with N_c=2048, C=512 (=> 4 chunks). Assert initial compute pointer == 0 and I/O pointer == 3. Step the schedule and assert compute pointer monotonically increases (0→1→2…) and I/O pointer monotonically decreases (3→2→1…) until they meet.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented so that token-wise recomputation and I/O transfer for different chunks execute concurrently (overlapped on separate streams) until the two pointers meet, at which point restoration terminates without redundant work.

verification.spec: Run a token-wise restoration for a request with 8 chunks while instrumenting the issued operations: assert recomputation and I/O loads are dispatched on separate CUDA streams (or async tasks) and that their timeline overlaps (start time of an I/O load occurs before the previous recompute finishes for at least one pair). Assert termination occurs when compute_ptr >= io_ptr with no chunk processed twice (no redundant work).

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented to ensure that token-wise recomputation respects the causal attention dependency: when recomputing chunk k, attention can attend to all prior chunks (including those already restored via I/O).

verification.spec: Set up a token-wise restoration where chunks [0..k-1] are recomputed and chunks [k..end] are loaded from cache. Run a forward pass on a small transformer and compare the resulting KV cache and final hidden states against a fully-recomputed reference. Assert numerical equivalence (max abs diff < 1e-3 or relative < 1e-4), confirming that recomputation of chunk k correctly attends to all prior chunks (those previously loaded via I/O are visible in the KV cache).

sub_tasks.requirements: Layer-wise parallelism (the second 2D axis) has been implemented as described in Section 3.1.
Code has been implemented to initialize a forward layer pointer l_fwd at layer 0 and a reverse layer pointer l_io at layer L-1, where L is the number of transformer layers.

verification.spec: Instantiate the layer-wise two-pointer restoration for a model config with L=32 layers. Assert initial l_fwd == 0 and l_io == 31. Repeat with L=28 and assert l_fwd==0, l_io==27.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented to recompute KV states bottom-up by advancing l_fwd through layers 0,1,2,... while concurrently loading KV states top-down by retreating l_io through layers L-1, L-2, ... from external storage.

verification.spec: Run layer-wise restoration on a small model and instrument: assert that l_fwd advances 0→1→2… executing recompute on those layers, while l_io retreats L-1→L-2… issuing loads on those layers, and that recompute and load operations on disjoint layers occur concurrently (on different streams/tasks). Assert a cutover layer ℓ is detected (where l_fwd > l_io) and loading terminates afterward with no overlap of restored layers.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented to identify a cutover layer l where the forward recomputation meets the loaded layers, and to terminate loading at that point so each layer's KV is restored exactly once.

verification.spec: Drive the layer-wise restoration on a small transformer (e.g., L=12) with instrumented compute and I/O pointers. After restoration, assert (a) every layer's KV is restored exactly once (counter==1 for each layer), (b) the cutover layer l reported by the system satisfies that layers [0,l) were recomputed and [l,L) were loaded with no overlap, (c) loading was terminated (no further I/O calls) after the cutover was reached.

sub_tasks.requirements: An adaptive parallelism selector that switches between token-wise and layer-wise restoration based on a profiled threshold L_delta has been implemented.
Code has been implemented for an offline profiling routine that, for the target hardware and model, measures T_token(N) and T_layer(N) across a range of sequence lengths.

verification.spec: Invoke the offline profiling entrypoint with a target model+hardware. Verify it produces a table mapping sequence length N -> (T_token(N), T_layer(N)) for at least 5 distinct lengths spanning short (<=1k) and long (>=10k). Check both columns are positive monotone-ish numbers with reasonable units (seconds or ms).

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented to compute the crossover threshold L_delta = min{ N | T_token(N) <= T_layer(N) } from the profiling results.

verification.spec: Feed a synthetic profiling table where T_token(N) and T_layer(N) cross at a known N* into the threshold-computation function. Assert it returns L_delta == N*. Also test edge cases: token-wise always faster (returns smallest N), and layer-wise always faster (returns sentinel / max).

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented so that at runtime each request selects token-wise restoration if its cached prefix length N_c >= L_delta and layer-wise restoration otherwise (matching Algorithm 1, line 2).

verification.spec: Set the configured L_delta to a known value (e.g., 4096). Construct two synthetic requests with N_c=8192 and N_c=512 and pass them through the scheduler's strategy-selection function. Assert the first returns 'token-wise' and the second returns 'layer-wise'. Vary L_delta and confirm the boundary behavior matches Algorithm 1 line 2.

sub_tasks.requirements: Multi-GPU parallelism (the third dimension) has been implemented as described in Section 3.2.
Code has been implemented to partition the transformer's L layers across S pipeline stages, with each stage s owning layers [l_start_s, l_end_s) on a dedicated GPU.

verification.spec: Initialize the system with L=32 layers and S=4 stages. Query the stage->layer mapping and assert (a) the union of [l_start_s, l_end_s) over all s equals [0,L), (b) the intervals are disjoint, (c) each stage is assigned to a distinct GPU device id, (d) sizes are balanced (max-min <= 1).

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented to cache and retrieve boundary activations (the input hidden state to the first layer of each stage) per request, which is much smaller than the per-stage KV cache.

verification.spec: Run a request through the offload pipeline with pipeline_stages=2 and capture what is persisted to the cache backend. Assert that, in addition to (or instead of) per-layer KV, a 'boundary activation' tensor is stored per stage with shape [seq_len, hidden_size] (i.e., the input to the first layer of each non-initial stage). Assert its size is roughly hidden_size/(2*L_per_stage*head_dim*num_kv_heads) times the KV cache size for that stage (i.e., much smaller).

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented so that each GPU stage independently and concurrently restores the KV cache for its local shard using the cached boundary activations, eliminating sequential cross-stage dependencies during restoration.

verification.spec: On a 2-stage pipeline deployment (e.g., 2xL40S or simulated CUDA streams), trigger KV restoration for a cached request and capture per-stage timestamps. Assert that stage 1 and stage 2 restoration windows overlap (start_2 < end_1) and that stage 2 begins restoration using cached boundary activations (does not wait for stage 1 to finish recomputing). Verify final KV equivalence vs. a sequential recompute reference within numerical tolerance.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented so that within each GPU stage the two-pointer (token-wise or layer-wise) mechanism is re-applied locally, yielding approximately T_comp/S and T_io/S per-stage costs.

verification.spec: Run multi-GPU restoration on a small model with S=2 and S=4 stages on the same workload. Measure per-stage restoration time. Assert each stage's local restoration time approximately equals T_two_pointer(T_comp/S, T_io/S) within 25%, and that overall restoration time decreases roughly as 1/S.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented so that after KV cache restoration completes, CacheFlow proceeds with standard prefill on the uncached suffix and produces the first output token, integrating seamlessly with the vLLM/LMCache serving pipeline.

verification.spec: End-to-end: send a request with a cached prefix and a fresh suffix to the CacheFlow-integrated vLLM server. Verify (a) KV restoration completes (logs/metrics confirm restoration phase ended), (b) the server then runs prefill on only the uncached suffix tokens (not re-prefilling the prefix), (c) the first token is emitted and matches (or has acceptable divergence from) the output of a vanilla vLLM run on the full prompt.

sub_tasks.requirements: Baseline systems used for comparison have been set up and made runnable on the same workloads.
Code has been implemented to run the vLLM baseline that restores KV cache purely via prefill recomputation (compute-bound extreme).

verification.spec: Launch the vLLM baseline against a request with cached prefix. Verify via logs/profiler that NO KV is loaded from external store (I/O bytes == 0) and that full prefill recomputation occurs over all N_c+suffix tokens. Confirm the first token is emitted successfully.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented to run the SGLang baseline (with HiCache extension of RadixAttention to storage tiers) as a state-of-the-art serving framework comparison.

verification.spec: Launch the SGLang baseline with HiCache enabled (RadixAttention extended to storage tiers). Send two requests sharing a common prefix; verify (a) the first request populates the radix cache including a storage-tier entry, (b) the second request hits the cache (logs show cache hit / storage-tier read) rather than fully recomputing, (c) the first token is emitted.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented to run the LMCache v0.3.1 baseline that performs pure KV cache loading from external storage with no recomputation (I/O-bound extreme).

verification.spec: Install LMCache v0.3.1 and run an end-to-end serving job that restores KV cache exclusively by loading from external (CPU/remote) storage with no recomputation path enabled. Verify by issuing a request whose prefix exists in the external KV store and confirming via logs/metrics that recomputation FLOPs are zero and that the first-token output is correct.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented to run the Cake baseline, which performs a per-request hybrid restoration that partitions the prefix along the token dimension only.

verification.spec: Implement the Cake hybrid restoration scheme that for each request partitions the cached prefix along the token dimension into a recompute region [0,k) and a load region [k,Nc), executes recomputation and I/O loading concurrently, and meets in the middle. Run on a sample request and verify that both compute and I/O are active concurrently and that restoration completes correctly (output tokens match a full-prefill reference).

sub_tasks.requirements: Evaluation metrics and measurement infrastructure have been implemented.
Code has been implemented to measure per-request Time-To-First-Token (TTFT) and produce CDFs of TTFT across requests.

verification.spec: Implement a measurement harness that for each request records the wall-clock time from request submission to emission of the first output token (TTFT), aggregates these across a workload, and emits a CDF (sorted TTFT values with empirical cumulative probabilities). Verify by running on a synthetic workload of >=100 requests and checking the CDF is monotonic in [0,1] and TTFT values are positive.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented to compute tail latencies (P90, P99) of TTFT from the per-request measurements.

verification.spec: Implement a function that, given a list of per-request TTFT measurements, computes the P50, P90, and P99 percentiles using numpy.percentile. Verify on a synthetic distribution with known percentiles that the returned values match within 1e-6.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented to measure average GPU compute utilization during KV cache restoration.

verification.spec: Implement a profiler that samples GPU compute utilization (e.g., via nvidia-smi pmon or NVML) at a fixed interval during the KV cache restoration phase only, and reports the average utilization (%). Verify on a known compute-heavy kernel (e.g., a large matmul) that utilization is >80% and on an idle GPU that it is <5%.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented to measure average I/O (PCIe / network) bandwidth utilization during KV cache restoration.

verification.spec: Implement a profiler that samples PCIe RX bandwidth (or network ingress for remote-tier KV) via NVML / dcgm / /proc/net/dev during the KV cache restoration phase and reports average bandwidth utilization as a percentage of the link's theoretical max. Verify on a controlled KV transfer of known size and duration that the reported utilization matches transferred_bytes/(duration*link_bw) within 5%.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented to characterize KV cache size as a function of input sequence length per model (Figure 1b), confirming linear growth and quick exhaustion of GPU memory at long contexts.

verification.spec: Run a script that, for Qwen3-8B, computes the KV cache size in GB as a function of input sequence length using formula 2 * L * H * d * N * dtype_bytes, where L, H, d are extracted from the model config (HuggingFace 'Qwen/Qwen3-8B'). Sweep N from 1K to 128K tokens and produce a plot/CSV matching Figure 1b. Verify that at N=20000 tokens, the reported KV cache size for Qwen3-8B is ~2.8 GB (within tolerance). Confirm linear scaling with N (R^2 > 0.99 of linear fit).

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented to measure pure recomputation latency and pure I/O restoration latency as functions of input sequence length (Figure 1c), demonstrating superlinear recomputation cost and bandwidth-sensitive I/O cost (e.g., at 10 Gbps and 40 Gbps).

verification.spec: Run benchmarks on an H100 with Qwen3-8B (or Llama-3.1-8B) loaded via vLLM/HuggingFace. (1) Measure prefill recomputation latency (TTFT, no KV reuse) across input sequence lengths {500, 2000, 8000, 32000, 128000}, confirming superlinear (quadratic-dominant) growth — fitting a*N + b*N^2 should yield b>0 with quadratic term contributing >30% at N=128K. (2) Measure pure I/O KV cache load latency by transferring a precomputed KV cache tensor over throttled links at 10 Gbps and 40 Gbps (use tc/netem or in-process rate limiting), confirming approximately linear scaling with N and that 10 Gbps latency exceeds recomputation latency at long contexts. Verify recomputation TTFT at ~128K tokens is ~1.35 s (within tolerance) on H100.

sub_tasks.requirements: The end-to-end TTFT experiments in Section 4.2 have been executed and their reported numerical claims have been measured for each workload/model.
The qualitative claim that CacheFlow's TTFT improvement is most pronounced on LMSys-Chat and SWE-Bench (longer-context workloads) has been verified from the measured CDFs.

verification.spec: From the measured TTFT CDFs of Figure 4 (WildChat, LMSys-Chat, SWE-Bench), compute the percentage TTFT reduction of CacheFlow vs. the best baseline at P50 on each workload. Verify the reduction on LMSys-Chat and SWE-Bench is larger than the reduction on WildChat, confirming the qualitative claim.

sub_tasks.requirements: The qualitative claim that the TTFT improvement widens in the tail (P90-P99) has been verified by reporting per-percentile improvement against the best baseline.

verification.spec: From the TTFT measurements collected in the end-to-end experiments, compute per-percentile (P50, P75, P90, P95, P99) speedup of CacheFlow over the best baseline. Verify the speedup at P90 and P99 is strictly greater than at P50, confirming the tail-latency widening claim.

sub_tasks.requirements: The resource-utilization experiment in Section 4.2 / Figure 5 has been reproduced.
The GPU compute utilization of vLLM (~91%) during KV restoration has been measured (paper_value=91, paper_unit=%).

verification.spec: Run vLLM serving Qwen3-8B on H100 with KV cache restoration via recomputation during the WildChat (or equivalent) workload. Sample GPU compute utilization (via nvidia-smi or DCGM SM-active counter) during the KV-restoration window at >=1Hz and report mean. Assert mean GPU utilization ≈ 91% within tolerance.

sub_tasks.requirements: The PCIe RX / I/O utilization of vLLM (~0%) during KV restoration has been measured (paper_value=0, paper_unit=%).

verification.spec: Run vLLM in pure-recomputation mode on Qwen3-8B during the KV cache restoration phase and sample PCIe RX bandwidth utilization. Verify the average is approximately 0% (vLLM does no KV loading).

sub_tasks.requirements: The GPU compute utilization of LMCache (~10%) during KV restoration has been measured (paper_value=10, paper_unit=%).

verification.spec: Run LMCache (loading-only, no recomputation) serving Qwen3-8B on H100 at 10 Gbps I/O during the KV-restoration window of the WildChat workload. Sample GPU compute (SM-active) utilization at >=1Hz; report mean. Assert mean GPU compute utilization ≈ 10% within tolerance.

sub_tasks.requirements: The I/O utilization of LMCache (~100%, saturated) during KV restoration has been measured (paper_value=100, paper_unit=%).

verification.spec: Run LMCache serving Qwen3-8B on H100 at 10 Gbps I/O. Measure PCIe RX / network RX throughput during KV-restoration windows (via /sys/class/net or nvidia-smi dmon for PCIe) and compute (measured_bw / configured_bw) * 100. Assert I/O utilization ≈ 100% (saturated) within tolerance.

sub_tasks.requirements: The GPU compute utilization of CacheFlow (~88%) during KV restoration has been measured (paper_value=88, paper_unit=%).

verification.spec: Run CacheFlow on Qwen3-8B with WildChat/LMSys-Chat workload at 10 Gbps I/O on a single H100 GPU. Sample GPU compute utilization (e.g., via nvidia-smi dmon or NVML SM utilization counter at 100 ms intervals) during the KV cache restoration phase across at least 100 requests and report the mean. Assert the mean GPU compute utilization is approximately 88%.

sub_tasks.requirements: The I/O utilization of CacheFlow (~78%) during KV restoration has been measured (paper_value=78, paper_unit=%).

verification.spec: Run CacheFlow on Qwen3-8B with WildChat/LMSys-Chat workload at 10 Gbps I/O on a single H100 GPU. Measure PCIe/NIC RX bandwidth utilization (e.g., via nvidia-smi pcie counters or NIC stats) during KV cache restoration across at least 100 requests, normalize against the available 10 Gbps channel, and report the mean. Assert mean I/O utilization is approximately 78%.

sub_tasks.requirements: The GPU compute utilization of Cake (~45%) during KV restoration has been measured (paper_value=45, paper_unit=%, tolerance=moderate).

verification.spec: Run the Cake baseline on Qwen3-8B and sample GPU compute utilization during the KV cache restoration phase using NVML. Verify the average is approximately 45%.

sub_tasks.requirements: The I/O utilization of Cake (~22%) during KV restoration has been measured (paper_value=22, paper_unit=%, tolerance=moderate).

verification.spec: Run the Cake baseline on Qwen3-8B and sample PCIe RX bandwidth utilization (% of link capacity) during the KV cache restoration phase. Verify the average is approximately 22%.

sub_tasks.requirements: Ablation studies in Section 4.3 have been executed.
The TTFT-by-prompt-length breakdown (Figure 6, Qwen3-8B) has been produced, comparing vLLM, SGLang, Cake, and CacheFlow over a 0-30K-token prompt range.

verification.spec: Sweep prompt lengths from 0 to 30K tokens (e.g., 6K, 12K, 18K, 24K, 30K) on Qwen3-8B and measure TTFT for vLLM, SGLang, Cake, and CacheFlow at each length. Emit a plot of TTFT vs. prompt length and verify vLLM/SGLang exhibit superlinear growth while CacheFlow grows more gracefully, matching Figure 6.

sub_tasks.requirements: The claim that the TTFT gap between vLLM/SGLang and CacheFlow widens from approximately 1.1x at 6K tokens to 1.7x at 30K tokens has been measured (paper_category=claim, paper_value=1.1-1.7, paper_unit=x speedup).

verification.spec: From the TTFT-by-prompt-length measurements on Qwen3-8B, compute the ratio (TTFT_best_baseline / TTFT_CacheFlow) at 6K and at 30K tokens. Verify the ratio is approximately 1.1x at 6K and 1.7x at 30K (gap widens).

sub_tasks.requirements: The 3D-parallelism ablation (Figure 7) comparing CacheFlow vs. CacheFlow w/o Multi-GPU vs. vLLM has been produced as a TTFT CDF.

verification.spec: Run three configurations on the same workload/model: (1) vLLM, (2) CacheFlow with multi-GPU parallelism DISABLED (2D token+layer only with sequential cross-GPU dependencies), and (3) full CacheFlow. Measure per-request TTFT and emit overlaid CDFs. Verify full CacheFlow CDF is left of 'w/o Multi-GPU', which in turn is left of vLLM, matching Figure 7.

sub_tasks.requirements: The numerical claim that average restoration latency rises from 0.21 s (full CacheFlow) to 0.29 s (without multi-GPU parallelism), a 38% increase, has been measured (paper_value: 0.21 s, 0.29 s, 38%, tolerance: moderate).

verification.spec: Run CacheFlow end-to-end on a multi-GPU deployment (e.g., 2xL40S or pipeline-parallel H100) with the SWE-Bench workload, twice: (a) with full 3D parallelism (multi-GPU restoration enabled), (b) with multi-GPU restoration disabled so each GPU only does 2D token+layer parallelism with sequential cross-GPU dependency. Run for L in {16, 32, 64} layers.

verification.spec: Implement a unit test that, given synthetic T_comp and T_io values and S pipeline stages (e.g., S in {1, 2, 4, 8}), computes the per-stage two-pointer optimum (T_comp/S * T_io/S) / (T_comp/S + T_io/S) = T*/S and verifies the multi-GPU scheduler achieves restoration time within 10% of T_single_gpu / S, demonstrating near-linear speedup. Verify the speedup ratio T_single/T_multi is approximately S across the tested values.
